title: Euploca haitiensis
intro: Euploca haitiensis, also known as Haitian trumpet creeper, is a plant species belonging to the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its vining habit and showy trumpet-shaped flowers. Traditionally, Euploca haitiensis is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The bioactive constituents of Euploca haitiensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and diterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Euploca haitiensis is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used to make tinctures and salves, which are used to treat a variety of health issues.
taxonomy: Euploca haitiensis belongs to the family Boraginaceae and is a member of the genus Euploca. It is a species of flowering plant native to Haiti. The plant's classification also includes the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it in the subclass Asteridae and the phylum Angiosperms. Its classification reflects its relationship to other flowering plants in the same families and orders.
